items.name: experiment-impact-tracker
items.description: The experiment-impact-tracker is meant to be a simple drop-in method to track energy usage, carbon emissions, and compute utilization of your system.

items.name: CodeCarbon
items.description: A Python package for estimating and tracking the carbon emissions of compute-intensive code.

items.name: Coppers
items.description: Coppers is a test harness for Rust that can measure the evolution of power consumptions of a Rust program between different versions with the help of Intel RAPL.

items.name: oaklean
items.description: A tool for analyzing and optimizing the energy efficiency of Node.js applications.

items.name: Green Metrics Tool
items.description: The Green Metrics Tool is a developer tool indented for measuring the energy and CO2 consumption of software through a software life cycle analysis (SLCA).

items.name: Greenspector Studio
items.description: A proprietary solution for measuring the energy efficiency and carbon footprint of websites and mobile applications on real devices on the test bench of Greenspector.

items.name: BoaviztAPI
items.description: An open source API that provides environmental impact data for IT equipment, including carbon footprint, energy consumption, and resource depletion.

items.name: CarbonDB
items.description: CarbonDB collects and consolidates energy usage data from multiple sources within a company, providing a unified view of energy consumption. It accurately converts energy usage into carbon emissions, enabling precise tracking of a company's carbon footprint.

items.name: Cloud Carbon Footprint
items.description: An open source tool that provides visibility and tooling to measure, monitor, and reduce your cloud carbon emissions.

items.name: Green Cost Explorer for AWS
items.description: The Green Cost Explorer is a tool by The Green Web Foundation that helps visualize AWS cloud spending alongside carbon emissions data to support more sustainable cloud infrastructure decisions.

items.name: Li10 for AWS
items.description: Measures the emissions for AWS resources with Software Carbon Intensity methodology defined by the Green Software Foundation.

items.name: aether
items.description: A open telemetry exporter to calculate the carbon emissions generated by your cloud infrastructure.

items.name: KEIT
items.description: KEIT (Kubernetes Emissions Insights Tool) supplements the energy recording with Kepler with current grid carbon intensity values and embodied carbon emissions.

items.name: Kepler
items.description: Kepler (Kubernetes-based Efficient Power Level Exporter) uses eBPF to probe performance counters and other system stats, use ML models to estimate workload energy consumption based on these stats, and exports them as Prometheus metrics.

items.name: Eco CI
items.description: Eco CI is a project aimed at estimating energy consumption in continuous integration (CI) environments. It provides functionality to calculate the energy consumption of CI jobs based on the power consumption characteristics of the underlying hardware.

items.name: Power Hog
items.description: A tool for monitoring the power consumption of desktop applications.

items.name: Cloud Energy
items.description: Cloud Energy is an XGBoost & linear model based on the energy data from the SPECPower database for the cloud to estimate wattage consumption of server by just a few input variables.

items.name: CPU Energy Meter
items.description: CPU Energy Meter is a Linux tool that allows to monitor power consumption of Intel CPUs at fine time granularities.

items.name: Scaphandre
items.description: A monitoring agent dedicated to energy consumption metrics of bare metal hosts and qemu/kvm virtual machines.

items.name: Camunda Carbon Reductor
items.description: A tool for optimizing business processes to reduce carbon emissions. It supports Camunda 7 and Camunda 8.

items.name: Carbon-Aware-Computing.com
items.description: An API service and tool suite for carbon-aware computing that uses data from Energy-Charts.

items.name: Carbon Aware SDK
items.description: A software development kit for building carbon-aware applications that can shift workloads to times and regions with cleaner electricity.

items.name: Carbon Aware KEDA Operator
items.description: A Kubernetes operator that scales workloads based on carbon intensity data.

items.name: CLEVER
items.description: Container Level Energy-efficient VPA Recommender for Kubernetes

items.name: Karpenter
items.description: A Kubernetes node autoscaler that helps improve application availability and cluster efficiency.

items.name: KEDA
items.description: A Kubernetes-based event-driven autoscaler that can scale applications based on event metrics from various sources.

items.name: kube-green
items.description: A Kubernetes operator to reduce CO2 footprint by shutting down idle resources.

items.name: aDoctor
items.description: A tool for detecting energy code smells in Android applications.

items.name: GreenIT-Analysis
items.description: A browser extension for analyzing the websites for best practices.

items.name: CO2.js
items.description: A JavaScript library for estimating the carbon emissions of websites and web applications.

items.name: Ecograder
items.description: Ecograder analyzes websites for environmental impact by evaluating performance, design, development practices, and hosting, offering insights and recommendations to improve digital sustainability. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

items.name: Website Carbon Calculator
items.description: Website Carbon Calculator estimates the carbon emissions of a website by analyzing factors like data transfer size and energy source of the hosting provider. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.
